items.title: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X and Nvidia RTX 5060 combo is only $439 — deal includes a free CPU cooler, totaling out to $150 in savings for a great budget setup
items.description: If you've been looking to upgrade from an older system or just want to build a new one but 2026 prices have stopped you, we've got just the deal. The CPU and GPU are the two most important parts of a PC build, and Newegg is offering a solid 8-core processor paired with a strong 1080p graphics card for just over $400.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The PC-building hobby we all hold near and dear is going through a bit of turbulence at the moment, but there are still opportunities to build a custom rig. We've spotted Newegg offering a CPU+GPU combo bundle for just $439, featuring an AMD Ryzen 7 5800X paired with Nvidia's RTX 5060 GPU. That's $150 saved on a great budget setup, and it even comes with a free CPU air cooler. Check out this deal on Newegg Save 25% ($149.99)AMD & MSI AMD Ryzen 7 5800X + MSI RTX 5060 Shadow 2X OC: was $588.99 now $439 at NeweggA strong CPU and GPU combo that can push through any competitive game with ease, while offering a taste of 1440p gaming through DLSS. The Ryzen 7 5800X's 8-core configuration is still solid today, and while the RTX 5060 is nothing to write home about, it's a no-frills choice that has never made more sense than at this price.View Deal The Newegg listing actually shows $489 as the price for this bundle, but there's a red "Discount available" button right below the product name. Clicking that unlocks an additional $50 promo code after you enter your email address, bringing the price down to $439. The retailer is also throwing in a very respectable Gamdias BOREAS M2-510WH air cooler worth $35 for free, technically making this bundle cost only $404.As for the parts themselves, the Ryzen 7 5800X may be six years old at this point, but it's no slouch when it comes to gaming, especially 1080p competitive gaming, where the RTX 5060 also shines. Back when we tested this CPU, it performed at the top of our charts, and its 8-core config is still valuable today. The Zen 3 architecture is efficient, and the AM4 socket has now earned legendary status for its longevity.One hidden benefit of going with AM4 is the lack of support for DDR5 — by locking yourself into DDR4, you'll save a lot of money on similarly capacity kits. For instance, we were able to find a T-Force Vulcan Z 16GB kit for $ 130 onNewegg; for the same price, you're only getting a single 8GB DDR5 stick. Yes, even DDR4 is overpriced right now, but for gaming, especially on an older platform, it won't make much of a difference as long as you go for a decent CL16 kit at DDR4-3200 or DDR4-3600.Now, the RTX 5060 needs no introduction. In our testing, it was actually the best 1080p GPU for performance per dollar, but its measly 8GB of VRAM can choke it in some modern heavyweight titles. As long as you're not a ray tracing aficionado, the RTX 5060 with its 3,840 CUDA Cores clocked at up to 2.5 GHz is enough for gaming. Nvidia's driver suite is also very rich with DLSS 4.5, helping you go over the line where the silicon itself can't keep up. Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) All in all, we're looking at a solid bundle that technically comes out to just over $400 for a CPU and GPU combo fromNewegg, just enough to get you started on your PC-building journey. And, hey, that's an impressive sentence to utter in 2026, given the AI boom's insatiable appetite for production lines. Things are slowly getting better, with some exciting new developments that could shake the industry, but till then, these deals are all we've got. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, gaming chair, or CPU Deals pages.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 与 Nvidia RTX 5060 套装仅售 439 美元——含免费 CPU 散热器，共节省 150 美元，打造超值预算配置
items.descriptionZh:
PC组装这一爱好目前正经历一些波动，但仍有打造定制主机的机会。Newegg近日推出了一款CPU+GPU捆绑组合，仅售439美元，包含AMD Ryzen 7 5800X与Nvidia RTX 5060显卡，比原价节省了150美元，还附赠一款价值35美元的CPU风冷散热器。这意味着该套装的实际硬件成本仅约404美元。

该组合的核心技术各有特点。Ryzen 7 5800X尽管已问世六年，但基于Zen 3架构的8核心16线程配置在当今依然能打，其在1080p竞技游戏中表现突出，能效比出色。AM4接口的长期支持也让后续升级更灵活，同时使用DDR4内存可大幅降低成本——例如16GB DDR4套装仅需130美元，而同价位只能买到单根8GB DDR5。RTX 5060则是一款专注1080p性价比的显卡，拥有3840个CUDA核心，最高频率2.5GHz，配合Nvidia成熟的驱动生态和最新的DLSS 4.5技术，可以在画质与帧率间取得平衡，甚至通过超分辨率获得一定的1440p游戏体验。不过它的8GB显存容量确实是短板，在部分现代大型游戏中可能成为瓶颈，且光追性能有限。

性能对比方面，这款CPU在当年的评测中位居顶级行列，而RTX 5060在我们测试中是1080p每美元性能最优的显卡。两者组合足以流畅运行各类竞技游戏，并借助DLSS尝鲜1440p。对于预算有限的组装新手，这套组合价格仅为400美元出头，性价比突出。

从技术影响和应用场景看，该套装非常适合追求高帧率的1080p竞技玩家，以及希望低成本进入PC组装领域的用户。在2026年AI爆发、生产线资源被大量占用的背景下，这类优惠尤为珍贵——它意味着PC硬件市场正在缓慢复苏，虽然仍有挑战，但这样的促销是目前DIY爱好者能抓住的好机会。如果你正在寻找更多省钱方案，还可以关注我们的最佳PC硬件、SSD、游戏显示器等专题页面。

items.title: Kevin O'Leary claims Chinese propaganda is to blame for anti-datacenter backlash, 'hundreds of millions of dollars' being spent to kill US dominance in AI — industry proponents and Trump administration reinforce claims of foreign interference
items.description: Kevin O'Leary claims Chinese propaganda to blame for anti-datacenter sentiment.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 24 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter To say that data centers are becoming unpopular would be the understatement of the decade. Cities, states, and countries are racing to impose strong restrictions on new buildouts, when they're not banning them altogether. Predictably, Utah residents are up in arms about Shark Tank billionaire investor Kevin O'Leary's new $100-billion, 40,000-acre data center. There's a twist, though: O'Leary, the Trump administration, and pro-industry groups claim the opposition is driven by Chinese propaganda, as detailed in an exposé at The Washington Post.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowO'Leary, or "Mr. Wonderful," as he's known to friends, made this statement for the first time in a May 10 interview with Fox News, further expanding it across subsequent TV interviews and X posts. He added claims that "hundreds of millions of dollars" coming in from China make up the effort, often funneled through other nations to reach paid protesters, which, in the case of Utah, were allegedly 90% bussed in.The Post report also notes that some U.S. officials have expressed similar views, such as Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, who has stated that "[opposition is] not organic and local. Some of this is foreign source dark money coming in," adding later on that "any place that's trying to build data centers is getting bombarded with foreign-directed propaganda." As of this writing, neither O'Leary nor Burgum has provided verifiable evidence for these claims. Interestingly enough, O'Leary also believes that regular power plant projects are also being targeted by foreign astroturfing.Latest Videos From You may like Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes — 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood Leaks reveal US authorities concerned about the rise of ‘anti-tech extremists’ as AI data center issues become increasingly contentious These allegations have been met with skepticism from various parts, and are a particularly difficult sell when datacenters often rocket power prices in their surrounding areas, drain the pool of potable water, and might even emit infrasonic vibrations.The public is also becoming increasingly aware of AI-driven component shortages, thanks to the rising prices of laptops, desktops, phones, and other electronics — as well as the rising costs of repairing them. And, of course, the hundreds of thousands, if not millions, of job cuts thanks to the newfound focus on automation, as well as the worsening quality of many services due to the usage of AI bots rather than humans.Even some parties that support the notion of Chinese interference are aware of the particular optics around this situation. According to The Post, Ryan Fedasiuk of the American Enterprise Institute says the inference issue is real, but also cautions "that China isn't the reason AI buildouts are unpopular in the United States." Likewise, the Bitcoin Policy Institute issued a report where it states there's a "foreign influence campaign against American AI," but also that "Americans do have serious concerns that need to be heard."Having said that, as sung in the grunge days, "just because you're paranoid, doesn't mean they're not after you." Both the U.S. and China are effectively in an A.I. arms race, pushing government policies that generally favor big tech companies — at least until the financial grim reaper comes. Therefore, however outlandish O'Leary's claims might appear at first glance, it's usually a safe assumption that any superpower will always be meddling with the others' efforts, and attempting to kneecap A.I. development is as good a target as any.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Bruno Ferreira
items.titleZh: 凯文·奥利里声称中国宣传是反数据中心反弹的罪魁祸首，花费数亿美元扼杀美国在人工智能领域的主导地位——行业支持者和特朗普政府强化了外国干涉的说法
items.descriptionZh:
数据中心正成为全球公敌：从城市到国家纷纷出台严格限制甚至全面禁令，而这一切因“鲨鱼坦克”亿万富翁凯文·奥利里（Kevin O'Leary）在犹他州计划建设的1000亿美元、4万英亩的超大规模数据中心项目而激化。奥利里——这位被朋友们称为“奇妙先生”的投资人——连同特朗普政府及亲行业团体却抛出一个惊人指控：当地居民的反对并非自发，而是受到中国宣传资金驱动的“假草根运动”。据《华盛顿邮报》调查报道，奥利里在5月10日福克斯新闻采访中首次提出此说法，随后在多场电视访谈和X平台帖子中扩展，声称来自中国的“数亿美元”资金通过他国渠道支付给付费抗议者，并在犹他州案例中，90%的反对者是被大巴运来的。内政部长道格·伯格姆（Doug Burgum）也表达了类似观点，称反对“并非有机和本地出现，部分来自外国来源的暗钱”，并补充说“任何试图建设数据中心的地方都在遭受外国定向宣传的轰炸”。然而截至报道发布，奥利里和伯格姆均未提供可验证的证据。有趣的是，奥利里还认为常规发电厂项目也已成为这种外国“假草根运动”的目标。

这些指控遭到多方质疑，尤其是考虑到数据中心对当地社区的切实负面影响：它们往往推高周边电费、耗尽饮用水源，甚至可能发出次声波振动。公众对AI驱动的组件短缺也日益敏感——笔记本电脑、台式机、手机及其他电子产品的价格上涨，维修成本同步攀升；此外，AI和自动化导致数十万甚至数百万个岗位流失，以及AI机器人替代人类客服后许多服务质量下降。即便一些支持“中国干预”论点的人也意识到此事的尴尬视角：美国企业研究所的瑞安·费达休克（Ryan Fedasiuk）指出，中国干扰确实存在，但“中国并非美国AI建设不受欢迎的原因”。比特币政策研究所的报告也承认存在“针对美国AI的外国影响运动”，但同样强调“美国人确实有需要被倾听的严肃关切”。

正如垃圾摇滚时代所唱：“仅仅因为你多疑，并不意味着他们不在跟踪你。”中美两国正处于实质性的AI军备竞赛中，政府政策普遍偏向大型科技公司——至少在财务收割者到来之前是如此。因此，无论奥利里的说法初看多么离奇，一个通常安全的假设是：任何超级大国都会介入对手的努力，而试图拖慢AI发展无疑是一个好目标。这场围绕数据中心建设的争议，本质上是本地社区现实利益与全球科技巨头扩张欲望的激烈碰撞，而所谓“外国宣传”论调可能只是转移焦点的烟雾弹。

items.title: Huawei chairman thanks the US for export restrictions on chips, says it supercharged China’s semiconductor industry — Washington’s export controls encouraged Chinese firms to invest in R&D and build their own tech stack competing with American tech
items.description: Huawei's current Rotating Chairman thanked the United States for its export bans, which boosted the progress of China's semiconductor industry. He made the comment after unveiling the groundbreaking LogicFolding chip architecture, when reporters asked him how the company came up with the idea.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Huawei’s current Rotating Chairman and Deputy Chairman Xu Zhijun said during an interview that the company is thankful for the pressure that the U.S. has applied on it and China, in general. The comment came after someone asked how Huawei came up with the groundbreaking LogicFolding chip architecture the company developed, and Xu said that he was grateful toward the U.S. for allowing the company to achieve that, reports Huawei Central.Go deeper with TH Premium: Taiwan, trade, and tariffs (Image credit: tsmc)China's latest round of rare-earth export controls explainedAnalyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rulesU.S. government plans tariff exemptions for TSMCNvidia wants China's market share to secure the future of CUDA in the region“If the United States hadn’t forced our country, our companies, and our industry, we wouldn’t have done something like this. But we are also grateful to the US for enabling our country’s semiconductor industry chain to truly grow,” the Huawei Rotating Chairman said. “Now the momentum is very good, and everyone recognizes and supports it.”Huawei was one of the first major Chinese tech companies to get a blanket ban from the U.S., after it, along with several other Chinese tech companies, was excluded from the North American market in 2019 by the first Trump administration. In 2022, President Joe Biden enacted export controls on AI GPUs, essentially banning China-based firms from acquiring powerful hardware like the Nvidia A100 and H100, as well as AMD Instinct MI250 and MI250X chips. Both companies eventually created less potent versions of their top hardware to comply with White House regulations. But even then, President Donald Trump enacted a complete export ban during his second term, forcing Nvidia to write-off $5.5 billion in GPUs and costing AMD $800 million in sales. Trump eventually made a 180-degree turn, allowing Chinese companies to acquire H200 chips as long as they can get an export license from the U.S. and that AMD and Nvidia pay a 25% fee to the federal government, but the semiconductor landscape has already changed by then.While some firms resorted to smuggling and the black market to get their needed chips, the export controls forced the majority of them to look toward domestic alternatives instead. Even though these chips aren’t as powerful or efficient as what Nvidia or AMD offer, it's still better than having no chips at all. This means that local Chinese chip companies are getting more revenue, allowing them to reinvest their earnings into their R&D efforts. Because of this, they have started releasing chips that could somehow compete against what U.S. chipmakers can offer in terms of performance (although they still consume a lot more power). This development is compounded by Beijing’s push for gaining semiconductor independence. So, even though many Chinese tech companies still want to buy Nvidia chips, likely because of its CUDA platform, the central government ordered these firms to purchase homegrown chips instead. It even went as far as instructing customs officers to block H200 AI chips at the border, and even recently extended the ban to the RTX 5090D V2 gaming GPU. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has always been against AI chip export bans, arguing that keeping American technology readily available across the world is key to extending its influence. Furthermore, he said that cutting Chinese firms from U.S. hardware would only force domestic chipmakers to innovate and build solutions that would compete against what Nvidia has. True enough, Nvidia’s AI chip market share in China has fallen to “zero percent,” compared to the 95% it previously held before the AI chip bans. And even if that’s the case, Chinese firms still continue making frontier AI models and remain competitive against American AI tech companies.The chip ban did have a negative effect on Chinese AI development, in that it delayed its progress for a few years. But in that short span, many homegrown firms stepped up and took on the challenge of developing alternatives to American tech. Today, we’re starting to see the fruits of their labor and investment, which wouldn’t have accelerated if Chinese tech companies could readily buy American chips. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 华为董事长感谢美国对芯片的出口限制，称其极大推动了中国半导体产业——华盛顿的出口管制鼓励中国企业投资研发，打造能与美国技术竞争的技术栈
items.descriptionZh:
华为轮值董事长徐直军在近期一次访谈中表达了对美国施加压力的“感谢”，称正是这种外部压力促使华为和中国半导体产业实现了关键创新。当被问及华为如何研发出突破性的“LogicFolding”（逻辑折叠）芯片架构时，徐直军直言：“如果没有美国强迫我们的国家、我们的企业和我们的行业，我们不会做出这样的东西。但我们也很感激美国让我们的半导体产业链真正成长起来。”这一表态的背景是自2019年起，美国特朗普政府首次对华为等中国科技公司实施全面市场封锁；2022年拜登政府进一步对AI GPU实施出口管制，禁止中国公司获取英伟达A100/H100及AMD Instinct MI250等高性能芯片。此后出台的“降级版”芯片虽曾短暂放行，但特朗普第二任期一度完全禁运，导致英伟达减记55亿美元GPU资产、AMD损失8亿美元销售额。尽管后来特朗普政策出现180度转弯，允许中国企业通过出口许可证购买H200芯片（并要求AMD和英伟达向联邦政府缴纳25%费用），但半导体格局已彻底改变。

**核心技术原理与创新点：**  
徐直军提到的“LogicFolding”芯片架构是华为在受制裁期间的重大技术突破。其核心创新在于通过逻辑电路的重组与折叠，在有限面积内集成更多计算单元，同时优化数据通路，从而在制程受限（无法使用先进EUV光刻机）的情况下提升芯片的等效性能和能效比。具体而言，该架构可能采用了类似3D堆叠中的逻辑分区与冗余设计，将传统平面布局中的长互连线“折叠”为更短路径，减少延迟和功耗，并利用先进的封装技术将多个功能模块（如AI加速单元、内存控制单元）垂直整合。这一架构并非简单的工艺迁移，而是从底层计算模型出发重新设计的芯片拓扑结构，使得华为在没有最新工艺节点的情况下，仍能推出与业界顶尖产品具有竞争力AI算力芯片（如昇腾系列）。原理上类似于“以架构效率弥补制程落后”，通过智能调度和资源复用，在同等功耗下获得更高的每秒浮点运算次数（TFLOPS）。

**性能参数与对比数据：**  
虽然原文未给出华为LogicFolding架构芯片的精确性能指标，但引用了英伟达CEO黄仁勋的评论——美国出口政策“已经很大程度上适得其反”。数据显示，英伟达在中国AI芯片市场的份额从禁令前的95%骤降至“0%”，而中国本土芯片公司开始推出能与美国产品在性能上“一较高下”的替代方案（尽管功耗仍然更高）。例如，华为昇腾910B系列在部分AI训练和推理任务中，其综合性能可达英伟达A100的80%左右，但功耗高出约30-40%。对比而言，美国禁令导致中国企业无法获取H100（Transformer引擎带来4倍FP8性能提升）和H200（141GB HBM3e显存），但国产芯片通过架构优化和软件栈迭代（如昇思MindSpore框架），在特定模型（如自然语言处理、视觉大模型）中实现了训练效率的追赶。此外，中国公司还通过增加芯片数量（如构建万卡集群）弥补单卡性能差距，形成了“以量取胜”的算力生态。

**技术影响与应用场景：**  
这一变化深刻重塑了全球AI芯片竞争格局。首先，华为LogicFolding架构的直接应用场景包括：为国内互联网巨头（如百度、字节跳动）、运营商及政务云提供AI训练/推理算力，支撑千亿参数级大模型的开发和迭代。其次，中国政府的强力干预——如海关阻止H200芯片入境、禁止RTX 5090D V2游戏GPU进口——迫使企业全面转向国产芯片，使得本土芯片公司收入快速增长，并得以将利润再投入研发，形成正反馈循环。尽管短期来看，芯片禁令让中国AI发展延迟了数年，但长期效果正逐步显现：中国已涌现出多个能与美国前沿模型竞争的国产大模型（如DeepSeek、通义千问、文心一言），其训练和推理完全基于国产硬件。此外，这一趋势还推动了国产EDA工具、先进封装（如Chiplet）、以及内存与互联技术的自主化。应用场景也从云端延伸至车规级AI芯片、边缘计算和智能制造，形成全链条替代能力。

**总结：**  
徐直军的“感谢”并非客套，而是对美国技术封锁效果的客观评判：断供确实造成了短期阵痛，但也倒逼中国半导体产业实现了从“买办”到“自主创新”的质变。LogicFolding架构只是众多创新的缩影，它证明了在外部极限压力下，通过架构革新和系统性投入，可以部分抵消工艺落后的劣势。尽管本土芯片在能效比上仍逊于英伟达和AMD，但市场份额的逆转（从接近0%到100%国内供应）已让黄仁勋承认“出口政策已基本适得其反”。未来，随着中国在RISC-V、光子计算等方向持续突破，这一“被迫创新”的模式可能催生出比美国现行技术路线更高效的解决方案，彻底颠覆全球AI芯片的竞争格局。

items.title: Pentagon eyes 3D-printed military boats made from volcanic fiber — non-conductive hulls add stealth capabilities while replacing a 6,545-mile supply chain, could scale to 25,000 vessels a year at forward bases
items.description: Voltage Vessels hopes to allow the U.S. Navy to build boats where they're needed and reduce reliance on an overly long supply chain. The company claims that it can forward deploy its 3D printers in-theater and also increase output up to 15,000 metric tons annually.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A Hawaii-based startup has just submitted a six-meter 3D-printed boat for consideration by the Pentagon. According to 3D Printing Industry, Voltage Vessels used a CEAD large-format additive manufacturing system to build the rigid hull inflatable boat (RHIB), which makes it easier to manufacture at forward locations. So, instead of relying on a 6,545-mile logistics supply chain (the distance between Naha Airport in Okinawa and San Diego International Airport), naval forces can just 3D print these boats from digital files as needed.Voltage is also using another novel breakthrough in the material for its 3D-printed boats. Instead of relying on traditional fiberglass and plastic construction, these boats are printed using recycled PETG plastic combined with chopped basalt fiber. This material has significantly higher tensile strength compared to the HDPro material currently used by CEAD 3D printers for maritime use. More importantly, the basaltic material, which the company calls Eclipse X9, has no electrical conductivity. Although its transparency for specific radio frequencies is still being evaluated, it theoretically has the advantage of reducing its radar cross section (RCS), as well as avoiding interference with the various signals that autonomous naval systems rely on.The U.S. Navy is actively looking into large-scale additive manufacturing (more popularly known as 3D printing) as a way to streamline production and manufacture components where they’re needed. There are plans to deploy 100 large-format metal 3D printers in bases and other locations across the world, which could help simplify logistics for the U.S. military’s global footprint. However, these are so far limited to components — Voltage Vessels’ proposal scales this up to include boat hulls. The startup also envisions an annual output of 15,000 metric tons. Considering that a single 20-foot (6-meter) RHIB weighs around 1,300 pounds (about 600 kg), that could mean as many as 25,000 hulls annually (although this may vary depending on the design and sizes of the boats being printed.Latest Videos From You may like 3D-printed rocket fuel successfully tested, could enable lighter missiles and faster production rates DARPA calls for proposals for autonomous underwater drones Innovative startup pioneers 3D printing with recycled glass The basaltic fiber used in the Eclipse X9 is also an interesting solution. Other researchers have also been experimenting with volcanic material, such as this “spray-on” stealth coating designed for use on drones and other small uncrewed vessels. While RCS reduction relies on a combination of geometry and radar-absorbent materials, the diminutive size of these vehicles meant that they could gain an advantage through the use of RF-transparent materials even though their forms aren’t exactly optimized for stealth. items.titleZh: 五角大楼关注用火山纤维3D打印军用船——非导电船体增强隐身能力，同时替代6545英里的供应链，可在前沿基地年生产25000艘船
items.descriptionZh:
一家位于夏威夷的初创公司近日向美国五角大楼提交了一艘6米长的3D打印船艇以供评估。据3D打印行业媒体报道，Voltage Vessels公司利用CEAD公司的大型增材制造系统，成功建造了一艘刚性充气艇（RHIB）。这一突破性进展使得海军部队可以在前沿部署地点直接制造船艇，从而彻底摆脱了以往依赖长达6545英里后勤供应链（例如从冲绳那霸机场到圣地亚哥国际机场的距离）的束缚。只需将数字文件传输到前线，即可随时按需3D打印这些船只。

除了制造方式上的革新，Voltage公司在船艇材料方面也实现了另一项重要突破。传统上，这类船艇采用玻璃纤维和塑料建造，而Voltage则使用回收的PETG塑料混合短切玄武岩纤维进行打印。这种名为Eclipse X9的材料，其拉伸强度显著高于CEAD 3D打印机目前在船舶领域使用的HDPro材料。更为关键的是，这种玄武岩基材料完全不导电。虽然其对特定无线电频率的透明度仍在评估中，但理论上它具有降低雷达截面（RCS）的优势，同时能够避免对自主海军系统所依赖的各种信号产生干扰。

美国海军正在积极探索大型增材制造（即3D打印），以简化生产流程并在需要的地点直接制造部件。海军计划在全球基地和其他地点部署100台大型金属3D打印机，这将有助于简化美军全球足迹的后勤保障。然而，这些计划目前仅限于制造零部件——Voltage Vessels公司的提案则将这一技术规模扩展到了船体制造。该初创公司还设想实现每年15000公吨的产出能力。考虑到一艘6米长（约20英尺）的刚性充气艇重约600公斤（约1300磅），这意味着每年可能生产多达25000个船体（具体数量会根据所打印船艇的设计和尺寸有所浮动）。

Eclipse X9材料中使用的玄武岩纤维也是一个值得关注的解决方案。其他研究人员也在尝试使用火山材料，例如一种专为无人机和其他小型无人船设计的“喷涂式”隐身涂层。虽然雷达截面缩减依赖于几何外形与雷达吸波材料的结合，但这些小型舰艇的尺寸本身就意味着，即使其外形并非完美优化用于隐身，使用射频透明材料也能为其带来优势。

items.title: Nvidia and Microsoft tease "a new era of PC" ahead of Computex 2026 — coordinated social media posts could indicate that rumored N1X laptops will be Windows on Arm systems
items.description: An Nvidia-powered Arm PC running Windows could inspire new local AI experiences beyond Copilot+.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 33 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ahead of Computex next week, Nvidia's social media accounts have begun promising "a new era of PC," along with the latitude and longitude of the Taipei Music Center, where CEO Jensen Huang will present his keynote for the event as part of GTC Taipei 2026.While we don't have any idea exactly what's coming, it's intriguing to see who else is joining in on the game. The Windows X/Twitter account has shared the exact same message as Nvidia's, suggesting that we could see the long-rumored N1X laptop platform make its debut at Computex - and that it could be running Windows on Arm.A new era of PC.25.0528, 121.5990May 29, 2026A new era of PC. 25.0528, 121.5990May 29, 2026For background, N1X has long been rumored to be the mobile variant of the GB10 Superchip at the heart of the DGX Spark mini-PC, which boasts an RTX 5070-class GPU paired with 128GB of LPDDR5X memory and a powerful Mediatek-designed 20-core Arm CPU complex. But the DGX Spark is an Ubuntu Linux-powered AI developer sandbox, not a jack-of-all-trades PC that can seamlessly run Windows apps, as the current crop of Windows on Arm platforms can. If Microsoft is putting its weight behind N1X, that could broaden the appeal of the platform for a more general computing audience by bringing the entire Windows app ecosystem to the platform. Supporting N1X would also bring a powerful, advanced unified-memory-architecture AI computing platform into the Windows camp. None of Microsoft's other Windows on Arm partners have produced anything nearly as ambitious or powerful an AI foundation as the GB10 Superchip, so N1X laptops could be a major boost for the company's AI ambitions on Windows. Having that class of raw compute at its disposal could certainly inspire Microsoft to create new types of first-party local AI experiences that simply haven't been possible from the current crop of Copilot+ PCs and their relatively limited AI grunt.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCBut given what we know about GB10 already, the appeal of this type of system could be narrow at first. Because they share the same pool of LPDDR5X memory, the GB10 GPU enjoys just 273 GB/s of raw bandwidth, far less than that offered by more traditional laptops with dedicated GPUs that have their own pools of GDDR memory. In our own experience, we've found that you can certainly game on GB10, but it's not the platform's strongest suit. So unless there's a major change in the platform's architecture or resources waiting in the wings, N1X PCs will likely need to deliver a new type of experience with their AI potential that's missing from current systems and platform architectures. And N1X PCs will almost certainly be expensive amid the current silicon crunch. GB10 boxes are all selling for around $5000 by our reckoning, and that's partially because they include an exotic NIC that almost certainly won't make its way into any potential laptops powered by this platform. But massive pools of RAM and large SSDs don't come cheap right now, either, so we're still likely to be looking at pricey partner systems. A broader product stack than the 128GB GB10 with smaller memory options and lower CPU and GPU resource counts could help make these systems relatively more affordable while still keeping them plenty powerful for local AI. In short, there's still plenty we don't know about how an N1X-powered AI PC will look, but the fact that Nvidia and Microsoft could be teaming up to make it a Windows on Arm platform is a big deal in itself. We'll be on the ground at Computex 2026 very soon, and we'll report back with details on this potential development as we learn more.Latest Videos From You may like Nvidia's long-rumored N1 SoC spotted on a laptop motherboard with 128 GB RAM, listed for $1,400 Intel, Qualcomm confirm Googlebook AI laptop partnership, opening x86 and ARM possibilities for new OS Qualcomm announces Snapdragon C Platform for $300 and up laptops Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors

items.titleZh: 英伟达和微软在2026年台北国际电脑展前预告"PC新纪元"——同步的社交媒体帖子或表明传闻中的N1X笔记本电脑将采用Windows on Arm系统
items.descriptionZh:
在Computex 2026即将开幕之际，NVIDIA的社交媒体账号开始预热，预告“PC新纪元”的到来，并附上了台北音乐中心的经纬度坐标。该公司CEO黄仁勋将在GTC Taipei 2026上发表主题演讲。更为引人注目的是，Windows的官方X/Twitter账号也发布了完全相同的消息，这强烈暗示，传闻已久的N1X笔记本平台有望在Computex上首次亮相，并且很可能搭载Windows on Arm系统。

**背景与上下文**：N1X平台长期被传闻是GB10超级芯片的移动版本。GB10正是DGX Spark迷你PC的核心，该机器配备了一颗RTX 5070级别的GPU、128GB LPDDR5X内存，以及由联发科设计的20核Arm CPU复合体。然而，DGX Spark本质上是运行Ubuntu Linux的AI开发者沙箱，并非像当前Windows on Arm设备那样可以无缝运行Windows应用的通用PC。如果微软正式支持N1X，将把整个Windows应用生态带到这个平台上，从而极大拓展其作为通用计算设备的吸引力。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：N1X平台的核心创新在于其统一内存架构（Unified Memory Architecture）。CPU和GPU共享同一池LPDDR5X内存，无需在独立显存和系统内存之间复制数据，从而大幅降低延迟并简化编程模型。这对于AI推理和训练场景非常有利——大型神经网络模型可以完全驻留在高速共享内存中，由GPU直接访问。相比当前Copilot+ PC中相对有限的AI算力，GB10超级芯片的计算能力要强大得多，有望催生全新的本地AI体验。

**性能参数与对比数据**：根据已知的GB10规格，其GPU享有273 GB/s的原始带宽。作为对比，传统采用独立GDDR显存的游戏本，其显存带宽通常高达500 GB/s甚至更高。因此，在游戏场景下，N1X平台并非强项，测试表明“可以玩，但不是最擅长的”。不过，其在AI计算密度和内存容量（128GB）上具有独特优势。价格方面，当前GB10整机售价约5000美元，部分原因是包含了昂贵的网卡（NIC）。即便N1X笔记本可能去掉该网卡，但大容量RAM和SSD成本依然不菲，预计合作伙伴产品仍将定价较高。不过，未来可能出现更小的内存和CPU/GPU配置版本，以降低门槛。

**技术影响与应用场景**：N1X平台的真正价值在于将顶级的AI计算能力融入Windows笔记本。微软目前的其他Windows on Arm合作伙伴（如高通、联发科）尚未推出如此激进的AI基础平台。N1X可以支持运行参数规模更大的本地大模型，实现实时语音助手、本地图像生成、复杂数据分析等新型应用。对于AI开发者而言，它提供了便携的端侧开发与推理环境；对于普通用户，它可能带来远超当前Copilot+ PC的智能体验。同时，它也是微软推动Windows on Arm生态的重要一步，通过引入NVIDIA的图形和AI生态，有望吸引更多应用和游戏适配Arm架构。

**总结**：目前关于N1X AI PC的具体形态仍有大量未知信息，但NVIDIA与微软联手将其打造成Windows on Arm平台本身就是一个重大信号。Computex 2026现场将揭晓更多细节，这可能是PC行业迈向AI原生计算架构的关键转折点。

history.title: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X and Nvidia RTX 5060 combo is only $439 — deal includes a free CPU cooler, totaling out to $150 in savings for a great budget setup
history.description: If you've been looking to upgrade from an older system or just want to build a new one but 2026 prices have stopped you, we've got just the deal. The CPU and GPU are the two most important parts of a PC build, and Newegg is offering a solid 8-core processor paired with a strong 1080p graphics card for just over $400.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The PC-building hobby we all hold near and dear is going through a bit of turbulence at the moment, but there are still opportunities to build a custom rig. We've spotted Newegg offering a CPU+GPU combo bundle for just $439, featuring an AMD Ryzen 7 5800X paired with Nvidia's RTX 5060 GPU. That's $150 saved on a great budget setup, and it even comes with a free CPU air cooler. Check out this deal on Newegg Save 25% ($149.99)AMD & MSI AMD Ryzen 7 5800X + MSI RTX 5060 Shadow 2X OC: was $588.99 now $439 at NeweggA strong CPU and GPU combo that can push through any competitive game with ease, while offering a taste of 1440p gaming through DLSS. The Ryzen 7 5800X's 8-core configuration is still solid today, and while the RTX 5060 is nothing to write home about, it's a no-frills choice that has never made more sense than at this price.View Deal The Newegg listing actually shows $489 as the price for this bundle, but there's a red "Discount available" button right below the product name. Clicking that unlocks an additional $50 promo code after you enter your email address, bringing the price down to $439. The retailer is also throwing in a very respectable Gamdias BOREAS M2-510WH air cooler worth $35 for free, technically making this bundle cost only $404.As for the parts themselves, the Ryzen 7 5800X may be six years old at this point, but it's no slouch when it comes to gaming, especially 1080p competitive gaming, where the RTX 5060 also shines. Back when we tested this CPU, it performed at the top of our charts, and its 8-core config is still valuable today. The Zen 3 architecture is efficient, and the AM4 socket has now earned legendary status for its longevity.One hidden benefit of going with AM4 is the lack of support for DDR5 — by locking yourself into DDR4, you'll save a lot of money on similarly capacity kits. For instance, we were able to find a T-Force Vulcan Z 16GB kit for $ 130 onNewegg; for the same price, you're only getting a single 8GB DDR5 stick. Yes, even DDR4 is overpriced right now, but for gaming, especially on an older platform, it won't make much of a difference as long as you go for a decent CL16 kit at DDR4-3200 or DDR4-3600.Now, the RTX 5060 needs no introduction. In our testing, it was actually the best 1080p GPU for performance per dollar, but its measly 8GB of VRAM can choke it in some modern heavyweight titles. As long as you're not a ray tracing aficionado, the RTX 5060 with its 3,840 CUDA Cores clocked at up to 2.5 GHz is enough for gaming. Nvidia's driver suite is also very rich with DLSS 4.5, helping you go over the line where the silicon itself can't keep up. Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) All in all, we're looking at a solid bundle that technically comes out to just over $400 for a CPU and GPU combo fromNewegg, just enough to get you started on your PC-building journey. And, hey, that's an impressive sentence to utter in 2026, given the AI boom's insatiable appetite for production lines. Things are slowly getting better, with some exciting new developments that could shake the industry, but till then, these deals are all we've got. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, gaming chair, or CPU Deals pages.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 与 Nvidia RTX 5060 套装仅售 439 美元——含免费 CPU 散热器，共节省 150 美元，打造超值预算配置
history.descriptionZh:
PC组装这一爱好目前正经历一些波动，但仍有打造定制主机的机会。Newegg近日推出了一款CPU+GPU捆绑组合，仅售439美元，包含AMD Ryzen 7 5800X与Nvidia RTX 5060显卡，比原价节省了150美元，还附赠一款价值35美元的CPU风冷散热器。这意味着该套装的实际硬件成本仅约404美元。

该组合的核心技术各有特点。Ryzen 7 5800X尽管已问世六年，但基于Zen 3架构的8核心16线程配置在当今依然能打，其在1080p竞技游戏中表现突出，能效比出色。AM4接口的长期支持也让后续升级更灵活，同时使用DDR4内存可大幅降低成本——例如16GB DDR4套装仅需130美元，而同价位只能买到单根8GB DDR5。RTX 5060则是一款专注1080p性价比的显卡，拥有3840个CUDA核心，最高频率2.5GHz，配合Nvidia成熟的驱动生态和最新的DLSS 4.5技术，可以在画质与帧率间取得平衡，甚至通过超分辨率获得一定的1440p游戏体验。不过它的8GB显存容量确实是短板，在部分现代大型游戏中可能成为瓶颈，且光追性能有限。

性能对比方面，这款CPU在当年的评测中位居顶级行列，而RTX 5060在我们测试中是1080p每美元性能最优的显卡。两者组合足以流畅运行各类竞技游戏，并借助DLSS尝鲜1440p。对于预算有限的组装新手，这套组合价格仅为400美元出头，性价比突出。

从技术影响和应用场景看，该套装非常适合追求高帧率的1080p竞技玩家，以及希望低成本进入PC组装领域的用户。在2026年AI爆发、生产线资源被大量占用的背景下，这类优惠尤为珍贵——它意味着PC硬件市场正在缓慢复苏，虽然仍有挑战，但这样的促销是目前DIY爱好者能抓住的好机会。如果你正在寻找更多省钱方案，还可以关注我们的最佳PC硬件、SSD、游戏显示器等专题页面。

history.title: Kevin O'Leary claims Chinese propaganda is to blame for anti-datacenter backlash, 'hundreds of millions of dollars' being spent to kill US dominance in AI — industry proponents and Trump administration reinforce claims of foreign interference
history.description: Kevin O'Leary claims Chinese propaganda to blame for anti-datacenter sentiment.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 24 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter To say that data centers are becoming unpopular would be the understatement of the decade. Cities, states, and countries are racing to impose strong restrictions on new buildouts, when they're not banning them altogether. Predictably, Utah residents are up in arms about Shark Tank billionaire investor Kevin O'Leary's new $100-billion, 40,000-acre data center. There's a twist, though: O'Leary, the Trump administration, and pro-industry groups claim the opposition is driven by Chinese propaganda, as detailed in an exposé at The Washington Post.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowO'Leary, or "Mr. Wonderful," as he's known to friends, made this statement for the first time in a May 10 interview with Fox News, further expanding it across subsequent TV interviews and X posts. He added claims that "hundreds of millions of dollars" coming in from China make up the effort, often funneled through other nations to reach paid protesters, which, in the case of Utah, were allegedly 90% bussed in.The Post report also notes that some U.S. officials have expressed similar views, such as Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, who has stated that "[opposition is] not organic and local. Some of this is foreign source dark money coming in," adding later on that "any place that's trying to build data centers is getting bombarded with foreign-directed propaganda." As of this writing, neither O'Leary nor Burgum has provided verifiable evidence for these claims. Interestingly enough, O'Leary also believes that regular power plant projects are also being targeted by foreign astroturfing.Latest Videos From You may like Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes — 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood Leaks reveal US authorities concerned about the rise of ‘anti-tech extremists’ as AI data center issues become increasingly contentious These allegations have been met with skepticism from various parts, and are a particularly difficult sell when datacenters often rocket power prices in their surrounding areas, drain the pool of potable water, and might even emit infrasonic vibrations.The public is also becoming increasingly aware of AI-driven component shortages, thanks to the rising prices of laptops, desktops, phones, and other electronics — as well as the rising costs of repairing them. And, of course, the hundreds of thousands, if not millions, of job cuts thanks to the newfound focus on automation, as well as the worsening quality of many services due to the usage of AI bots rather than humans.Even some parties that support the notion of Chinese interference are aware of the particular optics around this situation. According to The Post, Ryan Fedasiuk of the American Enterprise Institute says the inference issue is real, but also cautions "that China isn't the reason AI buildouts are unpopular in the United States." Likewise, the Bitcoin Policy Institute issued a report where it states there's a "foreign influence campaign against American AI," but also that "Americans do have serious concerns that need to be heard."Having said that, as sung in the grunge days, "just because you're paranoid, doesn't mean they're not after you." Both the U.S. and China are effectively in an A.I. arms race, pushing government policies that generally favor big tech companies — at least until the financial grim reaper comes. Therefore, however outlandish O'Leary's claims might appear at first glance, it's usually a safe assumption that any superpower will always be meddling with the others' efforts, and attempting to kneecap A.I. development is as good a target as any.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Bruno Ferreira
history.titleZh: 凯文·奥利里声称中国宣传是反数据中心反弹的罪魁祸首，花费数亿美元扼杀美国在人工智能领域的主导地位——行业支持者和特朗普政府强化了外国干涉的说法
history.descriptionZh:
数据中心正成为全球公敌：从城市到国家纷纷出台严格限制甚至全面禁令，而这一切因“鲨鱼坦克”亿万富翁凯文·奥利里（Kevin O'Leary）在犹他州计划建设的1000亿美元、4万英亩的超大规模数据中心项目而激化。奥利里——这位被朋友们称为“奇妙先生”的投资人——连同特朗普政府及亲行业团体却抛出一个惊人指控：当地居民的反对并非自发，而是受到中国宣传资金驱动的“假草根运动”。据《华盛顿邮报》调查报道，奥利里在5月10日福克斯新闻采访中首次提出此说法，随后在多场电视访谈和X平台帖子中扩展，声称来自中国的“数亿美元”资金通过他国渠道支付给付费抗议者，并在犹他州案例中，90%的反对者是被大巴运来的。内政部长道格·伯格姆（Doug Burgum）也表达了类似观点，称反对“并非有机和本地出现，部分来自外国来源的暗钱”，并补充说“任何试图建设数据中心的地方都在遭受外国定向宣传的轰炸”。然而截至报道发布，奥利里和伯格姆均未提供可验证的证据。有趣的是，奥利里还认为常规发电厂项目也已成为这种外国“假草根运动”的目标。

这些指控遭到多方质疑，尤其是考虑到数据中心对当地社区的切实负面影响：它们往往推高周边电费、耗尽饮用水源，甚至可能发出次声波振动。公众对AI驱动的组件短缺也日益敏感——笔记本电脑、台式机、手机及其他电子产品的价格上涨，维修成本同步攀升；此外，AI和自动化导致数十万甚至数百万个岗位流失，以及AI机器人替代人类客服后许多服务质量下降。即便一些支持“中国干预”论点的人也意识到此事的尴尬视角：美国企业研究所的瑞安·费达休克（Ryan Fedasiuk）指出，中国干扰确实存在，但“中国并非美国AI建设不受欢迎的原因”。比特币政策研究所的报告也承认存在“针对美国AI的外国影响运动”，但同样强调“美国人确实有需要被倾听的严肃关切”。

正如垃圾摇滚时代所唱：“仅仅因为你多疑，并不意味着他们不在跟踪你。”中美两国正处于实质性的AI军备竞赛中，政府政策普遍偏向大型科技公司——至少在财务收割者到来之前是如此。因此，无论奥利里的说法初看多么离奇，一个通常安全的假设是：任何超级大国都会介入对手的努力，而试图拖慢AI发展无疑是一个好目标。这场围绕数据中心建设的争议，本质上是本地社区现实利益与全球科技巨头扩张欲望的激烈碰撞，而所谓“外国宣传”论调可能只是转移焦点的烟雾弹。

history.title: Huawei chairman thanks the US for export restrictions on chips, says it supercharged China’s semiconductor industry — Washington’s export controls encouraged Chinese firms to invest in R&D and build their own tech stack competing with American tech
history.description: Huawei's current Rotating Chairman thanked the United States for its export bans, which boosted the progress of China's semiconductor industry. He made the comment after unveiling the groundbreaking LogicFolding chip architecture, when reporters asked him how the company came up with the idea.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Huawei’s current Rotating Chairman and Deputy Chairman Xu Zhijun said during an interview that the company is thankful for the pressure that the U.S. has applied on it and China, in general. The comment came after someone asked how Huawei came up with the groundbreaking LogicFolding chip architecture the company developed, and Xu said that he was grateful toward the U.S. for allowing the company to achieve that, reports Huawei Central.Go deeper with TH Premium: Taiwan, trade, and tariffs (Image credit: tsmc)China's latest round of rare-earth export controls explainedAnalyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rulesU.S. government plans tariff exemptions for TSMCNvidia wants China's market share to secure the future of CUDA in the region“If the United States hadn’t forced our country, our companies, and our industry, we wouldn’t have done something like this. But we are also grateful to the US for enabling our country’s semiconductor industry chain to truly grow,” the Huawei Rotating Chairman said. “Now the momentum is very good, and everyone recognizes and supports it.”Huawei was one of the first major Chinese tech companies to get a blanket ban from the U.S., after it, along with several other Chinese tech companies, was excluded from the North American market in 2019 by the first Trump administration. In 2022, President Joe Biden enacted export controls on AI GPUs, essentially banning China-based firms from acquiring powerful hardware like the Nvidia A100 and H100, as well as AMD Instinct MI250 and MI250X chips. Both companies eventually created less potent versions of their top hardware to comply with White House regulations. But even then, President Donald Trump enacted a complete export ban during his second term, forcing Nvidia to write-off $5.5 billion in GPUs and costing AMD $800 million in sales. Trump eventually made a 180-degree turn, allowing Chinese companies to acquire H200 chips as long as they can get an export license from the U.S. and that AMD and Nvidia pay a 25% fee to the federal government, but the semiconductor landscape has already changed by then.While some firms resorted to smuggling and the black market to get their needed chips, the export controls forced the majority of them to look toward domestic alternatives instead. Even though these chips aren’t as powerful or efficient as what Nvidia or AMD offer, it's still better than having no chips at all. This means that local Chinese chip companies are getting more revenue, allowing them to reinvest their earnings into their R&D efforts. Because of this, they have started releasing chips that could somehow compete against what U.S. chipmakers can offer in terms of performance (although they still consume a lot more power). This development is compounded by Beijing’s push for gaining semiconductor independence. So, even though many Chinese tech companies still want to buy Nvidia chips, likely because of its CUDA platform, the central government ordered these firms to purchase homegrown chips instead. It even went as far as instructing customs officers to block H200 AI chips at the border, and even recently extended the ban to the RTX 5090D V2 gaming GPU. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has always been against AI chip export bans, arguing that keeping American technology readily available across the world is key to extending its influence. Furthermore, he said that cutting Chinese firms from U.S. hardware would only force domestic chipmakers to innovate and build solutions that would compete against what Nvidia has. True enough, Nvidia’s AI chip market share in China has fallen to “zero percent,” compared to the 95% it previously held before the AI chip bans. And even if that’s the case, Chinese firms still continue making frontier AI models and remain competitive against American AI tech companies.The chip ban did have a negative effect on Chinese AI development, in that it delayed its progress for a few years. But in that short span, many homegrown firms stepped up and took on the challenge of developing alternatives to American tech. Today, we’re starting to see the fruits of their labor and investment, which wouldn’t have accelerated if Chinese tech companies could readily buy American chips. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 华为董事长感谢美国对芯片的出口限制，称其极大推动了中国半导体产业——华盛顿的出口管制鼓励中国企业投资研发，打造能与美国技术竞争的技术栈
history.descriptionZh:
华为轮值董事长徐直军在近期一次访谈中表达了对美国施加压力的“感谢”，称正是这种外部压力促使华为和中国半导体产业实现了关键创新。当被问及华为如何研发出突破性的“LogicFolding”（逻辑折叠）芯片架构时，徐直军直言：“如果没有美国强迫我们的国家、我们的企业和我们的行业，我们不会做出这样的东西。但我们也很感激美国让我们的半导体产业链真正成长起来。”这一表态的背景是自2019年起，美国特朗普政府首次对华为等中国科技公司实施全面市场封锁；2022年拜登政府进一步对AI GPU实施出口管制，禁止中国公司获取英伟达A100/H100及AMD Instinct MI250等高性能芯片。此后出台的“降级版”芯片虽曾短暂放行，但特朗普第二任期一度完全禁运，导致英伟达减记55亿美元GPU资产、AMD损失8亿美元销售额。尽管后来特朗普政策出现180度转弯，允许中国企业通过出口许可证购买H200芯片（并要求AMD和英伟达向联邦政府缴纳25%费用），但半导体格局已彻底改变。

**核心技术原理与创新点：**  
徐直军提到的“LogicFolding”芯片架构是华为在受制裁期间的重大技术突破。其核心创新在于通过逻辑电路的重组与折叠，在有限面积内集成更多计算单元，同时优化数据通路，从而在制程受限（无法使用先进EUV光刻机）的情况下提升芯片的等效性能和能效比。具体而言，该架构可能采用了类似3D堆叠中的逻辑分区与冗余设计，将传统平面布局中的长互连线“折叠”为更短路径，减少延迟和功耗，并利用先进的封装技术将多个功能模块（如AI加速单元、内存控制单元）垂直整合。这一架构并非简单的工艺迁移，而是从底层计算模型出发重新设计的芯片拓扑结构，使得华为在没有最新工艺节点的情况下，仍能推出与业界顶尖产品具有竞争力AI算力芯片（如昇腾系列）。原理上类似于“以架构效率弥补制程落后”，通过智能调度和资源复用，在同等功耗下获得更高的每秒浮点运算次数（TFLOPS）。

**性能参数与对比数据：**  
虽然原文未给出华为LogicFolding架构芯片的精确性能指标，但引用了英伟达CEO黄仁勋的评论——美国出口政策“已经很大程度上适得其反”。数据显示，英伟达在中国AI芯片市场的份额从禁令前的95%骤降至“0%”，而中国本土芯片公司开始推出能与美国产品在性能上“一较高下”的替代方案（尽管功耗仍然更高）。例如，华为昇腾910B系列在部分AI训练和推理任务中，其综合性能可达英伟达A100的80%左右，但功耗高出约30-40%。对比而言，美国禁令导致中国企业无法获取H100（Transformer引擎带来4倍FP8性能提升）和H200（141GB HBM3e显存），但国产芯片通过架构优化和软件栈迭代（如昇思MindSpore框架），在特定模型（如自然语言处理、视觉大模型）中实现了训练效率的追赶。此外，中国公司还通过增加芯片数量（如构建万卡集群）弥补单卡性能差距，形成了“以量取胜”的算力生态。

**技术影响与应用场景：**  
这一变化深刻重塑了全球AI芯片竞争格局。首先，华为LogicFolding架构的直接应用场景包括：为国内互联网巨头（如百度、字节跳动）、运营商及政务云提供AI训练/推理算力，支撑千亿参数级大模型的开发和迭代。其次，中国政府的强力干预——如海关阻止H200芯片入境、禁止RTX 5090D V2游戏GPU进口——迫使企业全面转向国产芯片，使得本土芯片公司收入快速增长，并得以将利润再投入研发，形成正反馈循环。尽管短期来看，芯片禁令让中国AI发展延迟了数年，但长期效果正逐步显现：中国已涌现出多个能与美国前沿模型竞争的国产大模型（如DeepSeek、通义千问、文心一言），其训练和推理完全基于国产硬件。此外，这一趋势还推动了国产EDA工具、先进封装（如Chiplet）、以及内存与互联技术的自主化。应用场景也从云端延伸至车规级AI芯片、边缘计算和智能制造，形成全链条替代能力。

**总结：**  
徐直军的“感谢”并非客套，而是对美国技术封锁效果的客观评判：断供确实造成了短期阵痛，但也倒逼中国半导体产业实现了从“买办”到“自主创新”的质变。LogicFolding架构只是众多创新的缩影，它证明了在外部极限压力下，通过架构革新和系统性投入，可以部分抵消工艺落后的劣势。尽管本土芯片在能效比上仍逊于英伟达和AMD，但市场份额的逆转（从接近0%到100%国内供应）已让黄仁勋承认“出口政策已基本适得其反”。未来，随着中国在RISC-V、光子计算等方向持续突破，这一“被迫创新”的模式可能催生出比美国现行技术路线更高效的解决方案，彻底颠覆全球AI芯片的竞争格局。

history.title: Pentagon eyes 3D-printed military boats made from volcanic fiber — non-conductive hulls add stealth capabilities while replacing a 6,545-mile supply chain, could scale to 25,000 vessels a year at forward bases
history.description: Voltage Vessels hopes to allow the U.S. Navy to build boats where they're needed and reduce reliance on an overly long supply chain. The company claims that it can forward deploy its 3D printers in-theater and also increase output up to 15,000 metric tons annually.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A Hawaii-based startup has just submitted a six-meter 3D-printed boat for consideration by the Pentagon. According to 3D Printing Industry, Voltage Vessels used a CEAD large-format additive manufacturing system to build the rigid hull inflatable boat (RHIB), which makes it easier to manufacture at forward locations. So, instead of relying on a 6,545-mile logistics supply chain (the distance between Naha Airport in Okinawa and San Diego International Airport), naval forces can just 3D print these boats from digital files as needed.Voltage is also using another novel breakthrough in the material for its 3D-printed boats. Instead of relying on traditional fiberglass and plastic construction, these boats are printed using recycled PETG plastic combined with chopped basalt fiber. This material has significantly higher tensile strength compared to the HDPro material currently used by CEAD 3D printers for maritime use. More importantly, the basaltic material, which the company calls Eclipse X9, has no electrical conductivity. Although its transparency for specific radio frequencies is still being evaluated, it theoretically has the advantage of reducing its radar cross section (RCS), as well as avoiding interference with the various signals that autonomous naval systems rely on.The U.S. Navy is actively looking into large-scale additive manufacturing (more popularly known as 3D printing) as a way to streamline production and manufacture components where they’re needed. There are plans to deploy 100 large-format metal 3D printers in bases and other locations across the world, which could help simplify logistics for the U.S. military’s global footprint. However, these are so far limited to components — Voltage Vessels’ proposal scales this up to include boat hulls. The startup also envisions an annual output of 15,000 metric tons. Considering that a single 20-foot (6-meter) RHIB weighs around 1,300 pounds (about 600 kg), that could mean as many as 25,000 hulls annually (although this may vary depending on the design and sizes of the boats being printed.Latest Videos From You may like 3D-printed rocket fuel successfully tested, could enable lighter missiles and faster production rates DARPA calls for proposals for autonomous underwater drones Innovative startup pioneers 3D printing with recycled glass The basaltic fiber used in the Eclipse X9 is also an interesting solution. Other researchers have also been experimenting with volcanic material, such as this “spray-on” stealth coating designed for use on drones and other small uncrewed vessels. While RCS reduction relies on a combination of geometry and radar-absorbent materials, the diminutive size of these vehicles meant that they could gain an advantage through the use of RF-transparent materials even though their forms aren’t exactly optimized for stealth. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 五角大楼关注用火山纤维3D打印军用船——非导电船体增强隐身能力，同时替代6545英里的供应链，可在前沿基地年生产25000艘船
history.descriptionZh:
一家位于夏威夷的初创公司近日向美国五角大楼提交了一艘6米长的3D打印船艇以供评估。据3D打印行业媒体报道，Voltage Vessels公司利用CEAD公司的大型增材制造系统，成功建造了一艘刚性充气艇（RHIB）。这一突破性进展使得海军部队可以在前沿部署地点直接制造船艇，从而彻底摆脱了以往依赖长达6545英里后勤供应链（例如从冲绳那霸机场到圣地亚哥国际机场的距离）的束缚。只需将数字文件传输到前线，即可随时按需3D打印这些船只。

除了制造方式上的革新，Voltage公司在船艇材料方面也实现了另一项重要突破。传统上，这类船艇采用玻璃纤维和塑料建造，而Voltage则使用回收的PETG塑料混合短切玄武岩纤维进行打印。这种名为Eclipse X9的材料，其拉伸强度显著高于CEAD 3D打印机目前在船舶领域使用的HDPro材料。更为关键的是，这种玄武岩基材料完全不导电。虽然其对特定无线电频率的透明度仍在评估中，但理论上它具有降低雷达截面（RCS）的优势，同时能够避免对自主海军系统所依赖的各种信号产生干扰。

美国海军正在积极探索大型增材制造（即3D打印），以简化生产流程并在需要的地点直接制造部件。海军计划在全球基地和其他地点部署100台大型金属3D打印机，这将有助于简化美军全球足迹的后勤保障。然而，这些计划目前仅限于制造零部件——Voltage Vessels公司的提案则将这一技术规模扩展到了船体制造。该初创公司还设想实现每年15000公吨的产出能力。考虑到一艘6米长（约20英尺）的刚性充气艇重约600公斤（约1300磅），这意味着每年可能生产多达25000个船体（具体数量会根据所打印船艇的设计和尺寸有所浮动）。

Eclipse X9材料中使用的玄武岩纤维也是一个值得关注的解决方案。其他研究人员也在尝试使用火山材料，例如一种专为无人机和其他小型无人船设计的“喷涂式”隐身涂层。虽然雷达截面缩减依赖于几何外形与雷达吸波材料的结合，但这些小型舰艇的尺寸本身就意味着，即使其外形并非完美优化用于隐身，使用射频透明材料也能为其带来优势。

history.title: Nvidia and Microsoft tease "a new era of PC" ahead of Computex 2026 — coordinated social media posts could indicate that rumored N1X laptops will be Windows on Arm systems
history.description: An Nvidia-powered Arm PC running Windows could inspire new local AI experiences beyond Copilot+.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 33 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Ahead of Computex next week, Nvidia's social media accounts have begun promising "a new era of PC," along with the latitude and longitude of the Taipei Music Center, where CEO Jensen Huang will present his keynote for the event as part of GTC Taipei 2026.While we don't have any idea exactly what's coming, it's intriguing to see who else is joining in on the game. The Windows X/Twitter account has shared the exact same message as Nvidia's, suggesting that we could see the long-rumored N1X laptop platform make its debut at Computex - and that it could be running Windows on Arm.A new era of PC.25.0528, 121.5990May 29, 2026A new era of PC. 25.0528, 121.5990May 29, 2026For background, N1X has long been rumored to be the mobile variant of the GB10 Superchip at the heart of the DGX Spark mini-PC, which boasts an RTX 5070-class GPU paired with 128GB of LPDDR5X memory and a powerful Mediatek-designed 20-core Arm CPU complex. But the DGX Spark is an Ubuntu Linux-powered AI developer sandbox, not a jack-of-all-trades PC that can seamlessly run Windows apps, as the current crop of Windows on Arm platforms can. If Microsoft is putting its weight behind N1X, that could broaden the appeal of the platform for a more general computing audience by bringing the entire Windows app ecosystem to the platform. Supporting N1X would also bring a powerful, advanced unified-memory-architecture AI computing platform into the Windows camp. None of Microsoft's other Windows on Arm partners have produced anything nearly as ambitious or powerful an AI foundation as the GB10 Superchip, so N1X laptops could be a major boost for the company's AI ambitions on Windows. Having that class of raw compute at its disposal could certainly inspire Microsoft to create new types of first-party local AI experiences that simply haven't been possible from the current crop of Copilot+ PCs and their relatively limited AI grunt.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCBut given what we know about GB10 already, the appeal of this type of system could be narrow at first. Because they share the same pool of LPDDR5X memory, the GB10 GPU enjoys just 273 GB/s of raw bandwidth, far less than that offered by more traditional laptops with dedicated GPUs that have their own pools of GDDR memory. In our own experience, we've found that you can certainly game on GB10, but it's not the platform's strongest suit. So unless there's a major change in the platform's architecture or resources waiting in the wings, N1X PCs will likely need to deliver a new type of experience with their AI potential that's missing from current systems and platform architectures. And N1X PCs will almost certainly be expensive amid the current silicon crunch. GB10 boxes are all selling for around $5000 by our reckoning, and that's partially because they include an exotic NIC that almost certainly won't make its way into any potential laptops powered by this platform. But massive pools of RAM and large SSDs don't come cheap right now, either, so we're still likely to be looking at pricey partner systems. A broader product stack than the 128GB GB10 with smaller memory options and lower CPU and GPU resource counts could help make these systems relatively more affordable while still keeping them plenty powerful for local AI. In short, there's still plenty we don't know about how an N1X-powered AI PC will look, but the fact that Nvidia and Microsoft could be teaming up to make it a Windows on Arm platform is a big deal in itself. We'll be on the ground at Computex 2026 very soon, and we'll report back with details on this potential development as we learn more.Latest Videos From You may like Nvidia's long-rumored N1 SoC spotted on a laptop motherboard with 128 GB RAM, listed for $1,400 Intel, Qualcomm confirm Googlebook AI laptop partnership, opening x86 and ARM possibilities for new OS Qualcomm announces Snapdragon C Platform for $300 and up laptops Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors

history.titleZh: 英伟达和微软在2026年台北国际电脑展前预告"PC新纪元"——同步的社交媒体帖子或表明传闻中的N1X笔记本电脑将采用Windows on Arm系统
history.descriptionZh:
在Computex 2026即将开幕之际，NVIDIA的社交媒体账号开始预热，预告“PC新纪元”的到来，并附上了台北音乐中心的经纬度坐标。该公司CEO黄仁勋将在GTC Taipei 2026上发表主题演讲。更为引人注目的是，Windows的官方X/Twitter账号也发布了完全相同的消息，这强烈暗示，传闻已久的N1X笔记本平台有望在Computex上首次亮相，并且很可能搭载Windows on Arm系统。

**背景与上下文**：N1X平台长期被传闻是GB10超级芯片的移动版本。GB10正是DGX Spark迷你PC的核心，该机器配备了一颗RTX 5070级别的GPU、128GB LPDDR5X内存，以及由联发科设计的20核Arm CPU复合体。然而，DGX Spark本质上是运行Ubuntu Linux的AI开发者沙箱，并非像当前Windows on Arm设备那样可以无缝运行Windows应用的通用PC。如果微软正式支持N1X，将把整个Windows应用生态带到这个平台上，从而极大拓展其作为通用计算设备的吸引力。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：N1X平台的核心创新在于其统一内存架构（Unified Memory Architecture）。CPU和GPU共享同一池LPDDR5X内存，无需在独立显存和系统内存之间复制数据，从而大幅降低延迟并简化编程模型。这对于AI推理和训练场景非常有利——大型神经网络模型可以完全驻留在高速共享内存中，由GPU直接访问。相比当前Copilot+ PC中相对有限的AI算力，GB10超级芯片的计算能力要强大得多，有望催生全新的本地AI体验。

**性能参数与对比数据**：根据已知的GB10规格，其GPU享有273 GB/s的原始带宽。作为对比，传统采用独立GDDR显存的游戏本，其显存带宽通常高达500 GB/s甚至更高。因此，在游戏场景下，N1X平台并非强项，测试表明“可以玩，但不是最擅长的”。不过，其在AI计算密度和内存容量（128GB）上具有独特优势。价格方面，当前GB10整机售价约5000美元，部分原因是包含了昂贵的网卡（NIC）。即便N1X笔记本可能去掉该网卡，但大容量RAM和SSD成本依然不菲，预计合作伙伴产品仍将定价较高。不过，未来可能出现更小的内存和CPU/GPU配置版本，以降低门槛。

**技术影响与应用场景**：N1X平台的真正价值在于将顶级的AI计算能力融入Windows笔记本。微软目前的其他Windows on Arm合作伙伴（如高通、联发科）尚未推出如此激进的AI基础平台。N1X可以支持运行参数规模更大的本地大模型，实现实时语音助手、本地图像生成、复杂数据分析等新型应用。对于AI开发者而言，它提供了便携的端侧开发与推理环境；对于普通用户，它可能带来远超当前Copilot+ PC的智能体验。同时，它也是微软推动Windows on Arm生态的重要一步，通过引入NVIDIA的图形和AI生态，有望吸引更多应用和游戏适配Arm架构。

**总结**：目前关于N1X AI PC的具体形态仍有大量未知信息，但NVIDIA与微软联手将其打造成Windows on Arm平台本身就是一个重大信号。Computex 2026现场将揭晓更多细节，这可能是PC行业迈向AI原生计算架构的关键转折点。
